title: sambucus racemosa subsp. kamtschatica
intro: Sambucus racemosa subsp. kamtschatica, also known as the Kamchatka elderberry, is a subspecies of the Sambucus racemosa plant, a species of flowering plant in the family Adoxaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Sambucus racemosa subsp. kamtschatica include reducing the severity and duration of cold and flu symptoms, as well as alleviating pain and inflammation. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and capsules, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed in various forms.
